disclaimer: simulation is decision support, not deterministic forecast
method_note: This impact report is generated from mock interaction events for dashboard demonstration. Historical backtest credibility is based on blind_prediction.json, not these mock events.

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Mayor of London (Sadiq Khan)
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: policy salience and implementation risk are monitored

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Outer London residents
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: outer-London backlash is stronger than inner-London support groups

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Inner London residents
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: inner-London support is visible through air-quality and health framing

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: ULEZ opponents (radical subgroup)
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: enforcement backlash and sabotage narratives are visible

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Conservative Party (local and national)
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: policy salience and implementation risk are monitored

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Labour Party (local and national)
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: policy salience and implementation risk are monitored

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Transport for London (TfL)
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: policy salience and implementation risk are monitored

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Van drivers, tradespeople and small businesses
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: van-dependent tradespeople are a high-pressure economic group

stakeholder_impact_matrix.name: Low-income households dependent on private vehicles
stakeholder_impact_matrix.qualitative_signal: distributional burden and affordability concerns are visible

risk_timeline.signal: outer-London and van-dependent groups show strong backlash
political salience and enforcement backlash require active management
behavioural adaptation and compliance improvement emerge over time

mitigation_options.option: targeted support for van-dependent small businesses
mitigation_options.rationale: reduces the highest-impact economic pressure without changing stakeholder weights automatically
addresses distributional burden while preserving air-quality objectives

mitigation_options.option: visible enforcement legitimacy and camera-protection plan
mitigation_options.rationale: responds to enforcement backlash without escalating automated actions

confidence_notes: Backtest comparison is directional only and does not score exact historical percentages.
Political output is framed as political salience/electoral risk, not causal proof or election-result prediction.
Mock simulation is used for the 2026-07-01 MVP path; OASIS live simulation remains a stretch validation.
The R1-R6 backtest is scored from blind_prediction.json rather than mock simulation events.

system_signals.R1: outer-London and vehicle-dependent groups show clearly stronger opposition than inner-London support groups
system_signals.R2: van drivers, tradespeople and small businesses are singled out as high-impact, high-opposition economic groups
system_signals.R3: political salience and electoral risk are top risks; this is not a claim about a specific election result
system_signals.R4: enforcement backlash, sabotage narratives and camera resistance risk are visible
system_signals.R5: short-term opposition coexists with longer-term behavioural adaptation and improving compliance
system_signals.R6: air-quality and health benefits coexist with distributional burden for lower-income and vehicle-dependent groups
